name: Data from Local System
description: Read a small handful of world-readable local files that a post-foothold adversary would commonly stage for exfiltration: /etc/hostname, /etc/timezone, /etc/issue, $HOME/.profile (if present). MITRE ATT&CK T1005. The TTP captures content into the envelope's stdout (which IS the audit record) but performs no network exfil and writes nothing to disk beyond the envelope. Read-only. Safety: refuse-by-default unless --target <whitelisted_IP> or --unsafe-local.
exec: echo '--- /etc/hostname ---'; cat /etc/hostname 2>/dev/null || echo '(absent)'; echo '--- /etc/timezone ---'; cat /etc/timezone 2>/dev/null || echo '(absent or systemd-managed)'; echo '--- /etc/issue (first 5 lines) ---'; head -5 /etc/issue 2>/dev/null || echo '(absent)'; echo '--- $HOME/.profile (size only) ---'; [ -f "$HOME/.profile" ] && stat -c 'size_bytes=%s mtime=%Y' "$HOME/.profile" || echo '(absent)'
